claims.claim_text: The paper provides a public GitHub repository for CubiD.
gold_external_evidence_hints: A successful GitHub check should confirm that the repository URL named in the paper is reachable.

claims.claim_text: The repository evidence is strong enough to partially support the paper's reproducibility claim.
gold_external_evidence_hints: Repository existence and basic health signals should improve confidence, but they do not fully prove end-to-end reproducibility.

claims.claim_text: CubiD-XXL achieves 2.02 gFID on ImageNet 256x256 without guidance.
CubiD achieves state-of-the-art discrete generation results on ImageNet 256x256.

gold_evidence_spans.text: We achieve state-of-the-art discrete generation results on ImageNet 256x256.

gold_external_evidence_hints: The paper presents its own comparison table, but broader state-of-the-art status requires external literature context.

claims.claim_text: CubiD is the first discrete generation model for high-dimensional representations.

gold_evidence_spans.text: In this paper, we present Cubic Discrete Diffusion (CubiD), the first discrete generation model for high-dimensional representations.

gold_external_evidence_hints: A prior-work search is appropriate, but the current tools may still remain inconclusive on a strong first-of-its-kind claim.

claims.claim_text: The paper proposes a Quantum-Inspired Estimation of Distribution Algorithm (QIEDA) for the Travelling Salesman Problem.

gold_evidence_spans.text: A novel Quantum-Inspired Estimation of Distribution Algorithm (QIEDA) is proposed to solve the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP).

claims.claim_text: QIEDA is compared against EDA, PSO, GA, and ACO baselines across TSP sizes from 5 to 20.

gold_evidence_spans.text: The experiments have been carried out for sizes n = [5, 7, 10, 12, 15, 17, 20]. Figure 4 shows an analysis of the optimum solutions obtained by the QIEDA compared to other population-based algorithms.

claims.claim_text: The paper claims that quantum noise enhances the search for an optimal solution.

gold_evidence_spans.text: Moreover, we show that quantum noise enhances the search of an optimal solution.
Our results suggest that intrinsic quantum noise has the ability to enhance the convergence and the cost of the TSP.

claims.claim_text: QIEDA is a novel approach for solving the Travelling Salesman Problem.

gold_evidence_spans.text: A novel Quantum-Inspired Estimation of Distribution Algorithm (QIEDA) is proposed to solve the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP).

gold_external_evidence_hints: This is a strong novelty claim. External literature lookup is appropriate, but unresolved is acceptable if matches remain ambiguous.

claims.claim_text: HC-MPF groups highly correlated state variables within the same subspace and weakly correlated ones into separate subspaces.

gold_evidence_spans.text: This method groups highly correlated state variables within the same subspace while assigning weakly correlated ones to separate subspaces.

claims.claim_text: Numerical experiments demonstrate that HC-MPF outperforms existing partitioning techniques in nonlinear data assimilation applications.

gold_evidence_spans.text: Numerical experiments demonstrate that HC-MPF outperforms existing partitioning techniques in nonlinear DA applications.

claims.claim_text: The experiments use the Lorenz 96 model with system dimension dx = 40 and forcing constant F = 8.

gold_evidence_spans.text: The Lorenz 96 model is a low-dimensional chaotic system widely used in data assimilation. In the standard configuration, the system dimension is set to dx = 40 and the forcing constant is F = 8.

claims.claim_text: HC-MPF is a novel state partitioning scheme for MPF.

gold_evidence_spans.text: In this paper, we propose a hierarchical clustering-based state partitioning scheme for MPF (HC-MPF).

gold_external_evidence_hints: The paper clearly introduces the method, but external literature is still needed before treating the novelty claim as settled.
